class.name: PolygonTriangulation
class.brief_description: Low-level wrapper around the DMWT optimal 3D polygon triangulator.
class.description:
Exposes the DMWT (Discrete Minimum Weight Triangulation) algorithm from Ming Zou et al. (SGP 2013) with direct control over cost weights and solver parameters. For most use cases [PolygonTriangulationGodot] or [CassieTriangulator] are easier to work with.
Usage: construct via [method PolygonTriangulationGodot.create] or [method PolygonTriangulationGodot.create_planar], call [method preprocess] then [method start].
[codeblock] # Prefer PolygonTriangulationGodot for Godot-friendly output. # Use PolygonTriangulation directly when you need to tune DMWT internals. var pt := PolygonTriangulation._create_with_normals(n, pts_ptr, degen_ptr, norms_ptr, false) pt.set_weights(1.0, 0.5, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0) pt.preprocess() pt.start() [/codeblock]
Implemented as a thin GDScript-visible wrapper; the underlying DMWT object is accessed from C++ via [code]get_dmwt()[/code] (not exposed to GDScript).

link.title: An Algorithm for Triangulating Multiple 3D Polygons (Zou et al., SGP 2013)

method.name: clear_tiling
method.description: Clears the cached tiling result so [method start] can be called again with the same boundary.

method.name: preprocess
method.description: Runs the DMWT preprocessing stage: computes the Delaunay tetrahedralization of the boundary points and builds the candidate triangle set. Must be called before [method start].

method.name: set_dot
method.description: Enables or disables the dot-product normal heuristic during candidate triangle selection. Leave at the default unless you are reproducing specific results from the SGP 2013 paper.

method.name: set_point_limit
method.description: Caps the number of boundary points the algorithm processes. Inputs beyond [param limit] are ignored. Use this to bound worst-case computation time.

method.name: set_round
method.description: Sets the number of optimization rounds the dynamic programming solver performs. More rounds improve solution quality at the cost of time. Default is 1.

method.name: set_weights
method.description:
Sets the five additive cost weights for the DMWT objective function: [br]- [param wtri]: weight on total triangle area. [br]- [param wedge]: weight on total dihedral angle between adjacent triangles. [br]- [param wbitri]: weight on the bi-triangle dihedral angle term. [br]- [param wtribd]: weight on the triangle-boundary dihedral angle term. [br]- [param wwst]: weight on the worst single dihedral angle (minimax term).
All weights default to 0 except [param wedge] = 1. Setting [param wwst] > 0 produces smoother surfaces at higher computational cost.

method.name: start
method.description: Runs the DMWT dynamic programming solver. Returns [code]true[/code] on success. Call [method preprocess] first. Results are stored internally and retrieved via [PolygonTriangulationGodot] or the C++ [code]get_indexed_result()[/code] accessor.

method.name: statistics
method.description: Prints solver statistics to the Godot output log: triangle count, edge count, optimal cost, and whether the early-exit condition triggered.
